compiled_state.title: As an agent, the test gate excludes incoming-only files during rebase operations
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add REBASE_HEAD detection and incoming-only file filtering to pre-commit-test-gate.sh, mirroring the existing MERGE_HEAD handling pattern.
**Why**: During automated rebase operations (squash-rebase recovery, git pull --rebase), the test gate false-positives on files modified only on the rebase target. This is the core fix that unblocks agent workflows.
**Scope**:
- IN: REBASE_HEAD detection, onto ref reading (rebase-merge/onto and rebase-apply/onto), orig-head reading from rebase state directory, incoming-only file identification and exclusion, fail-open fallback paths, bash tests
- OUT: Other scripts with MERGE_HEAD filtering (covered by sibling story). Implementation approach for shared vs inline detection left to implementation planning.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the test gate excludes files changed only on the rebase target when REBASE_HEAD is present during a commit
  <- Satisfies: "When REBASE_HEAD is present during a commit, the test gate identifies files changed only on the rebase target (not by the worktree branch) and excludes them from gate enforcement"
- When this story is complete, files modified by both the worktree branch and the rebase target remain subject to gate enforcement
  <- Satisfies: "Files modified by both the worktree branch and the rebase target (conflicted and resolved) remain subject to gate enforcement"
- When this story is complete, the rebase detection reads the onto ref from .git/rebase-merge/onto or .git/rebase-apply/onto
  <- Satisfies: "The rebase detection reads the onto ref from .git/rebase-merge/onto or .git/rebase-apply/onto to compute the correct merge base"
- When this story is complete, the squash-rebase recovery path in merge-to-main.sh completes without test gate false-positives on incoming-only files
  <- Satisfies: "The squash-rebase recovery path in merge-to-main.sh completes without test gate false-positives on incoming-only files"
- When this story is complete, bash tests simulate REBASE_HEAD state with incoming-only files (gate passes), conflict-resolved files (gate enforces), and missing onto file (gate fails open)
  <- Satisfies: "Test suite includes bash tests that simulate REBASE_HEAD state"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Must fail open when onto file missing, ref unresolvable, or shallow clone lacks merge-base ancestor
- [Reliability] Must use git rev-parse --git-dir for per-worktree rebase state resolution in linked worktrees
- [Reliability] Must read orig-head from rebase state directory, not ORIG_HEAD ref (overwritten by subsequent operations)
- [Testing] Must mirror existing MERGE_HEAD test patterns in test harness
- [Maintainability] Follow MERGE_HEAD pattern closely for consistency with future 5ee9-143e refactoring
- [Consumer Impact] pre-commit-review-gate.sh has identical MERGE_HEAD filtering that also needs REBASE_HEAD support (covered by sibling story)
- [Implicit Shared State] record-test-status.sh mirrors test gate MERGE_HEAD filtering for hash computation; must filter identically during rebase (covered by sibling story)

comments.body: Story complete: REBASE_HEAD detection and incoming-only file filtering added to pre-commit-test-gate.sh. 4 behavioral tests cover incoming-only filtering, worktree-branch enforcement, failsafe on missing onto, and prune guard. All tests GREEN.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
